Cloudflare One
Get started
Replace your VPN
Network to network
Network to network
Connect two separate private networks so devices on each network can send and receive traffic in both directions through Cloudflare. This is useful when you need to link office locations, data centers, or cloud environments. For example, employees in one office could access a file server, printer, or internal application in another office.
To explore other connection scenarios, refer to Replace your VPN.
This guide follows the same steps as the Get Started experience in the Cloudflare One dashboard ↗.
How it works
WARP Connector Beta is a network connector that you install on a single Linux device in each network. That device handles traffic for the entire network: it sends outbound traffic to Cloudflare and receives inbound traffic back, then passes it to the right device on the network. Because of this, other devices on the network do not need to install any software.
Prerequisites
A Cloudflare account with a Zero Trust organization. If you have not set this up, refer to Get started.
A Linux device or virtual machine on your first private network. This is where you install your first WARP Connector.
A second Linux device or virtual machine on a separate private network. This is where you install your second WARP Connector.
WARP Connector is currently Linux-only. For more details on requirements and limitations, refer to WARP Connector.
Step 1: Define a network segment
A network segment identifies the IP range of a private network you want to connect. When you define a segment, the dashboard creates a WARP Connector configuration and sets up the routes that tell Cloudflare how to reach your network. You install and run the connector in the next step.
In Cloudflare One ↗, select the Get Started tab.
For Replace my client-based or site-to-site VPN, select Get started.
For Network to network, select Continue.
On the Route traffic between private networks screen, select Continue.
Enter the IP range of your first network segment (for example, 10.0.0.0/24).
Enter a name for this network segment (for example, office-a).
Select Continue.
If you are not sure of your network's IP range, check your router or network settings.
Step 2: Deploy first connector
Install the WARP Connector on a Linux device in your first network segment. The dashboard generates commands specific to your operating system.
Select your device's operating system from the dropdown.
Select your device's operating system from the dropdown.
Copy and run the commands shown in the dashboard on your Linux device. The dashboard provides three sets of commands:

Install WARP: Sets up the package repository and installs the cloudflare-warp package.
Enable IP forwarding: Allows the device to forward traffic between networks.
Run the WARP Connector with token: Registers the connector with your Cloudflare account and connects it.
Copy and run the commands shown in the dashboard on your Linux device. The dashboard provides three sets of commands:
Install WARP: Sets up the package repository and installs the cloudflare-warp package.
Enable IP forwarding: Allows the device to forward traffic between networks.
Run the WARP Connector with token: Registers the connector with your Cloudflare account and connects it.
After the connector deploys, the dashboard confirms your network segment is active.
After the connector deploys, the dashboard confirms your network segment is active.
Select Continue.
Select Continue.
Step 3: Define a second segment
Repeat the same process as Step 1 for your second network. The IP range must not overlap with your first segment. Each network needs its own unique range so Cloudflare can route traffic to the correct destination (for example, 10.0.1.0/24 if your first segment is 10.0.0.0/24).
Step 4: Deploy second connector
Repeat the same process as Step 2 on a Linux device in your second network segment. After the connector deploys, the dashboard confirms your network segment is active.
Step 5: Forward device traffic
If the WARP Connector is installed on your network's router (the device that serves as the default gateway), other devices on the network automatically send traffic through it. No additional configuration is needed, and you can skip this step.
If the WARP Connector is installed on a different device, other devices on the network need a static route so they know to send cross-network traffic to the WARP Connector device. Without this route, devices do not know where to send traffic destined for the other network, and the connection does not work. The dashboard provides OS-specific commands for the devices you want to forward traffic from.
Select the operating system of the device you want to configure.
Select the tunnel you want to route traffic through.
Copy and run the generated command on the target device.
Repeat for additional devices as needed, or select Continue to proceed to the final step.
For more details on routing options, refer to Connect two or more private networks.
Step 6: Verify your connection
The dashboard confirms that your connectors can reach devices in the opposite network segment. Devices on both networks can now communicate through Cloudflare.
To verify connectivity, try reaching a device on the opposite network (for example, ping 10.0.1.100 from a device on your first network).
Recommended next steps
After verifying your connection, consider securing your connected networks with policies and access controls:
Set up Gateway policies: By default, all traffic between your network segments flows through Cloudflare without restriction. Gateway policies let you scan, filter, and log traffic between your networks. For more information, refer to DNS policies, Network policies, and HTTP policies.
Create an Access application: Restrict access to specific services or hosts on your connected networks with identity-based rules. For more information, refer to Secure a private IP or hostname.
Create a device profile: Control which traffic your connectors route through Cloudflare independently from your user devices. For more information, refer to Connect two or more private networks.
Explore more with Zero Trust: Review your connectors, policies, and routes in the Cloudflare One dashboard ↗.
For in-depth guidance on policy design and device posture checks, refer to the Replace your VPN learning path.
Troubleshoot
If you have issues connecting, refer to these resources:
Tips and best practices: review common WARP Connector configuration tips and troubleshooting strategies.
Troubleshoot tunnels: diagnose tunnel connectivity and routing problems.

	window.StarlightThemeProvider = (() => {
		const storedTheme =
			typeof localStorage !== 'undefined' && localStorage.getItem('starlight-theme');
		const theme =
			storedTheme ||
			(window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: light)').matches ? 'light' : 'dark');
		document.documentElement.dataset.theme = theme === 'light' ? 'light' : 'dark';
		return {
			updatePickers(theme = storedTheme || 'auto') {
				document.querySelectorAll('starlight-theme-select').forEach((picker) => {
					const select = picker.querySelector('select');
					if (select) select.value = theme;
					/** @type {HTMLTemplateElement | null} */
					const tmpl = document.querySelector(`#theme-icons`);
					const newIcon = tmpl && tmpl.content.querySelector('.' + theme);
					if (newIcon) {
						const oldIcon = picker.querySelector('svg.label-icon');
						if (oldIcon) {
							oldIcon.replaceChildren(...newIcon.cloneNode(true).childNodes);
						}
					}
				});
			},
		};
	})();
</script><template id="theme-icons"><svg aria-hidden="true" class="light astro-c6vsoqas" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor" style="--sl-icon-size: 1em;"><path d="M5 12a1 1 0 0 0-1-1H3a1 1 0 0 0 0 2h1a1 1 0 0 0 1-1Zm.64 5-.71.71a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.41 1 1 0 0 0 1.41 0l.71-.71A1 1 0 0 0 5.64 17ZM12 5a1 1 0 0 0 1-1V3a1 1 0 0 0-2 0v1a1 1 0 0 0 1 1Zm5.66 2.34a1 1 0 0 0 .7-.29l.71-.71a1 1 0 1 0-1.41-1.41l-.66.71a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.41 1 1 0 0 0 .66.29Zm-12-.29a1 1 0 0 0 1.41 0 1 1 0 0 0 0-1.41l-.71-.71a1.004 1.004 0 1 0-1.43 1.41l.73.71ZM21 11h-1a1 1 0 0 0 0 2h1a1 1 0 0 0 0-2Zm-2.64 6A1 1 0 0 0 17 18.36l.71.71a1 1 0 0 0 1.41 0 1 1 0 0 0 0-1.41l-.76-.66ZM12 6.5a5.5 5.5 0 1 0 5.5 5.5A5.51 5.51 0 0 0 12 6.5Zm0 9a3.5 3.5 0 1 1 0-7 3.5 3.5 0 0 1 0 7Zm0 3.5a1 1 0 0 0-1 1v1a1 1 0 0 0 2 0v-1a1 1 0 0 0-1-1Z"/></svg><svg aria-hidden="true" class="dark astro-c6vsoqas" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor" style="--sl-icon-size: 1em;"><path d="M21.64 13a1 1 0 0 0-1.05-.14 8.049 8.049 0 0 1-3.37.73 8.15 8.15 0 0 1-8.14-8.1 8.59 8.59 0 0 1 .25-2A1 1 0 0 0 8 2.36a10.14 10.14 0 1 0 14 11.69 1 1 0 0 0-.36-1.05Zm-9.5 6.69A8.14 8.14 0 0 1 7.08 5.22v.27a10.15 10.15 0 0 0 10.14 10.14 9.784 9.784 0 0 0 2.1-.22 8.11 8.11 0 0 1-7.18 4.32v-.04Z"/></svg><svg aria-hidden="true" class="auto astro-c6vsoqas" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="currentColor" style="--sl-icon-size: 1em;"><path d="M21 14h-1V7a3 3 0 0 0-3-3H7a3 3 0 0 0-3 3v7H3a1 1 0 0 0-1 1v2a3 3 0 0 0 3 3h14a3 3 0 0 0 3-3v-2a1 1 0 0 0-1-1ZM6 7a1 1 0 0 1 1-1h10a1 1 0 0 1 1 1v7H6V7Zm14 10a1 1 0 0 1-1 1H5a1 1 0 0 1-1-1v-1h16v1Z"/></svg></template><link rel="stylesheet" href="/_astro/print.DNXP8c50.css" media="print"><link rel="stylesheet" href="/_astro/tailwind.Ojy6sf0r.css">

<div class="sl-markdown-content astro-cedpceuv">  <p>Connect two separate private networks so devices on each network can send and receive traffic in both directions through Cloudflare. This is useful when you need to link office locations, data centers, or cloud environments. For example, employees in one office could access a file server, printer, or internal application in another office.</p>
<p>To explore other connection scenarios, refer to <a href="/cloudflare-one/setup/replace-vpn/">Replace your VPN</a>.</p>
<p>This guide follows the same steps as the <strong>Get Started</strong> experience in the <a href="https://one.dash.cloudflare.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Cloudflare One dashboard<span class="external-link"> ↗</span></a>.</p>
<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="how-it-works">How it works</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#how-it-works"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<p><a href="/cloudflare-one/networks/connectors/cloudflare-tunnel/private-net/warp-connector/">WARP Connector</a> <span class="sl-badge caution small inline astro-avdet4wd">Beta</span> is a network connector that you install on a single Linux device in each network. That device handles traffic for the entire network: it sends outbound traffic to Cloudflare and receives inbound traffic back, then passes it to the right device on the network. Because of this, other devices on the network do not need to install any software.</p>
<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="prerequisites">Prerequisites</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#prerequisites"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<ul>
<li>A Cloudflare account with a Zero Trust organization. If you have not set this up, refer to <a href="/cloudflare-one/setup/">Get started</a>.</li>
<li>A Linux device or virtual machine on your first private network. This is where you install your first WARP Connector.</li>
<li>A second Linux device or virtual machine on a separate private network. This is where you install your second WARP Connector.</li>
</ul>

<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="step-1-define-a-network-segment">Step 1: Define a network segment</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#step-1-define-a-network-segment"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<p>A network segment identifies the IP range of a private network you want to connect. When you define a segment, the dashboard creates a WARP Connector configuration and sets up the routes that tell Cloudflare how to reach your network. You install and run the connector in the next step.</p>
<ol>
<li>In <a href="https://one.dash.cloudflare.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Cloudflare One<span class="external-link"> ↗</span></a>, select the <strong>Get Started</strong> tab.</li>
<li>For <strong>Replace my client-based or site-to-site VPN</strong>, select <strong>Get started</strong>.</li>
<li>For <strong>Network to network</strong>, select <strong>Continue</strong>.</li>
<li>On the <strong>Route traffic between private networks</strong> screen, select <strong>Continue</strong>.</li>
<li>Enter the IP range of your first network segment (for example, <code dir="auto">10.0.0.0/24</code>).</li>
<li>Enter a name for this network segment (for example, <code dir="auto">office-a</code>).</li>
<li>Select <strong>Continue</strong>.</li>
</ol>

<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="step-2-deploy-first-connector">Step 2: Deploy first connector</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#step-2-deploy-first-connector"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<p>Install the WARP Connector on a Linux device in your first network segment. The dashboard generates commands specific to your operating system.</p>
<ol>
<li>
<p>Select your device's operating system from the dropdown.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Copy and run the commands shown in the dashboard on your Linux device. The dashboard provides three sets of commands:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Install WARP</strong>: Sets up the package repository and installs the <code dir="auto">cloudflare-warp</code> package.</li>
<li><strong>Enable IP forwarding</strong>: Allows the device to forward traffic between networks.</li>
<li><strong>Run the WARP Connector with token</strong>: Registers the connector with your Cloudflare account and connects it.</li>
</ol>
</li>
<li>
<p>After the connector deploys, the dashboard confirms your network segment is active.</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Select <strong>Continue</strong>.</p>
</li>
</ol>
<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="step-3-define-a-second-segment">Step 3: Define a second segment</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#step-3-define-a-second-segment"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<p>Repeat the same process as <a href="#step-1-define-a-network-segment">Step 1</a> for your second network. The IP range must not overlap with your first segment. Each network needs its own unique range so Cloudflare can route traffic to the correct destination (for example, <code dir="auto">10.0.1.0/24</code> if your first segment is <code dir="auto">10.0.0.0/24</code>).</p>
<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="step-4-deploy-second-connector">Step 4: Deploy second connector</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#step-4-deploy-second-connector"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<p>Repeat the same process as <a href="#step-2-deploy-first-connector">Step 2</a> on a Linux device in your second network segment. After the connector deploys, the dashboard confirms your network segment is active.</p>
<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="step-5-forward-device-traffic">Step 5: Forward device traffic</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#step-5-forward-device-traffic"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<p>If the WARP Connector is installed on your network's router (the device that serves as the default gateway), other devices on the network automatically send traffic through it. No additional configuration is needed, and you can skip this step.</p>
<p>If the WARP Connector is installed on a different device, other devices on the network need a static route so they know to send cross-network traffic to the WARP Connector device. Without this route, devices do not know where to send traffic destined for the other network, and the connection does not work. The dashboard provides OS-specific commands for the devices you want to forward traffic from.</p>
<ol>
<li>Select the operating system of the device you want to configure.</li>
<li>Select the tunnel you want to route traffic through.</li>
<li>Copy and run the generated command on the target device.</li>
<li>Repeat for additional devices as needed, or select <strong>Continue</strong> to proceed to the final step.</li>
</ol>
<p>For more details on routing options, refer to <a href="/cloudflare-one/networks/connectors/cloudflare-tunnel/private-net/warp-connector/site-to-site/#4-route-traffic-from-subnet-to-warp-connector">Connect two or more private networks</a>.</p>
<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="step-6-verify-your-connection">Step 6: Verify your connection</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#step-6-verify-your-connection"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<p>The dashboard confirms that your connectors can reach devices in the opposite network segment. Devices on both networks can now communicate through Cloudflare.</p>
<p>To verify connectivity, try reaching a device on the opposite network (for example, <code dir="auto">ping 10.0.1.100</code> from a device on your first network).</p>
<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="recommended-next-steps">Recommended next steps</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#recommended-next-steps"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<p>After verifying your connection, consider securing your connected networks with policies and access controls:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Set up Gateway policies</strong>: By default, all traffic between your network segments flows through Cloudflare without restriction. Gateway policies let you scan, filter, and log traffic between your networks. For more information, refer to <a href="/cloudflare-one/traffic-policies/dns-policies/">DNS policies</a>, <a href="/cloudflare-one/traffic-policies/network-policies/">Network policies</a>, and <a href="/cloudflare-one/traffic-policies/http-policies/">HTTP policies</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Create an Access application</strong>: Restrict access to specific services or hosts on your connected networks with identity-based rules. For more information, refer to <a href="/cloudflare-one/access-controls/applications/non-http/self-hosted-private-app/">Secure a private IP or hostname</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Create a device profile</strong>: Control which traffic your connectors route through Cloudflare independently from your user devices. For more information, refer to <a href="/cloudflare-one/networks/connectors/cloudflare-tunnel/private-net/warp-connector/site-to-site/">Connect two or more private networks</a>.</li>
<li><strong>Explore more with Zero Trust</strong>: Review your connectors, policies, and routes in the <a href="https://one.dash.cloudflare.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Cloudflare One dashboard<span class="external-link"> ↗</span></a>.</li>
</ul>
<p>For in-depth guidance on policy design and device posture checks, refer to the <a href="/learning-paths/replace-vpn/concepts/">Replace your VPN learning path</a>.</p>
<div tabindex="-1" class="heading-wrapper level-h2"><h2 id="troubleshoot">Troubleshoot</h2><a class="anchor-link" href="#troubleshoot"><span aria-hidden="true" class="anchor-icon"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path fill="currentcolor" d="m12.11 15.39-3.88 3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1-3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0-3.5l3.88-3.88a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-3.88 3.89a4.48 4.48 0 0 0 6.33 6.33l3.89-3.88a1 1 0 1 0-1.42-1.42Zm8.58-12.08a4.49 4.49 0 0 0-6.33 0l-3.89 3.88a1 1 0 0 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.88a2.52 2.52 0 0 1 3.5 0 2.47 2.47 0 0 1 0 3.5l-3.88 3.88a1 1 0 1 0 1.42 1.42l3.88-3.89a4.49 4.49 0 0 0 0-6.33ZM8.83 15.17a1 1 0 0 0 1.1.22 1 1 0 0 0 .32-.22l4.92-4.92a1 1 0 0 0-1.42-1.42l-4.92 4.92a1 1 0 0 0 0 1.42Z"></path></svg></span></a></div>
<p>If you have issues connecting, refer to these resources:</p>
<ul>
<li><a href="/cloudflare-one/networks/connectors/cloudflare-tunnel/private-net/warp-connector/tips/">Tips and best practices</a>: review common WARP Connector configuration tips and troubleshooting strategies.</li>
<li><a href="/cloudflare-one/networks/connectors/cloudflare-tunnel/troubleshoot-tunnels/">Troubleshoot tunnels</a>: diagnose tunnel connectivity and routing problems.</li>
</ul>  </div>  <footer class="sl-flex astro-fxeopwe4"> <div class="custom-footer-section w-full astro-fxeopwe4"> <div class="grid grid-cols-2 gap-8 pt-6 pb-10 lg:grid-cols-5 astro-fxeopwe4"> <ul class="flex w-full list-none flex-col gap-3 pl-0 astro-fxeopwe4"> <li class="text-xs leading-5 astro-fxeopwe4"> <strong class="astro-fxeopwe4">Resources</strong> </li> <li class="text-sm astro-fxeopwe4"> <a href="/api/" class="flex items-center gap-2 text-black!
